title: Streaming Lakehouse — Engineering Post
rawText: This article covers building a streaming lakehouse: partitioning strategies for even load, watermarks for event-time windows, exactly-once sinks, and periodic compaction of small files in object storage.

teachCards.title: Streaming Topology Basics
summary: Sources, transforms, and sinks form a directed acyclic flow.
Backpressure propagates upstream when sinks slow down.

teachCards.title: Z-Order for Lake Queries
summary: It speeds multi-predicate filters on large tables.
Trade write cost for cheaper selective scans.
teachCards.diagramMermaid:
flowchart TD
  Rows --> Z[Z-Order Clustering]
  Z --> Fast[Faster multi-column filters]

quiz.prompt: Why combine partitioning with Z-order in a lakehouse?
choices: To eliminate the need for watermarks
Partition prunes files; Z-order improves locality inside partitions
